false_positives_removed: gui-001 — process() runs on main thread via self.after(100, process), NOT in a background thread. self.after() schedules callbacks on the Tkinter main event loop. Widget updates ARE thread-safe because they execute on the main thread.
gui-002 — Same false positive as gui-001. self.progress.set() called via self.after() on main thread.
gui-013 — Same false positive. self.doc_count_label.configure() called via self.after() on main thread.
gui-014 — Same false positive. self.ask_button.configure() and question_entry.configure() called via self.after() on main thread.

findings.title: Unvalidated numeric input in settings save — ValueError crash
findings.problem: In _save(), int() and float() conversions (lines 169-174) are called without try/except. If user enters non-numeric values in chunk_size, n_results, max_tokens, temperature, or retrieval_window fields, ValueError crashes the settings dialog with no user-friendly error message.
findings.fix: Wrap each conversion in try/except with messagebox.showerror() for user-friendly feedback. Alternatively, validate all fields before building the result dict.

findings.title: God class: DocumentQAApp is 552 lines with no separation of concerns
findings.problem: DocumentQAApp class mixes UI creation, event handlers, engine management, threading, message processing, and settings persistence — all in one 552-line class. This violates Single Responsibility Principle and makes testing, maintenance, and code review difficult.
findings.fix: Extract into separate classes/modules: EngineManager (engine lifecycle), MessageProcessor (queue consumption), SettingsManager (settings persistence), UI views (chat panel, settings dialog).
findings.ai_pattern: AI-generated monolith — all code in one class

findings.title: Dead code: get_resource_path() never called
findings.problem: The function get_resource_path() (lines 36-42) is defined but never invoked anywhere in the codebase. If it was intended for PyInstaller resource loading, it's unused dead code.
findings.fix: Remove the function if truly unused, or integrate it into resource loading paths if needed.

findings.title: Unused import: datetime
findings.problem: from datetime import datetime at line 14 is never used anywhere in the file.

findings.title: Silent exception swallowing in settings loading
findings.problem: Settings loading is wrapped in broad 'except Exception: pass' (or similar). JSON parsing failures, file corruption, or permission errors are silently swallowed. User has no indication that settings failed to load and defaults were used instead.
findings.fix: Log the exception or show a warning: logger.warning('Failed to load settings: %s', e)

findings.title: No URL format validation in settings
findings.problem: API URL and Ollama URL fields accept any text without validating URL format. Invalid URLs will cause connection errors at runtime with unclear feedback to the user.
findings.fix: Add URL validation using urllib.parse.urlparse() before saving: verify scheme is http/https and hostname is present.

findings.title: Magic number: conversation history limit hardcoded to 20
findings.problem: Conversation history truncation uses a hardcoded limit of 20 messages. This should be a class constant or configurable value.

findings.title: Class definitions outside try/except — NameError if customtkinter missing
findings.problem: SettingsDialog (line 45) and DocumentQAApp (line 183) inherit from CTkToplevel and CTk, which are defined inside the try/except block at lines 16-22. If customtkinter is not installed, GUI_AVAILABLE=False but the class definitions still execute, causing NameError. The GUI_AVAILABLE flag is never checked before defining classes.
findings.fix: Move class definitions inside the try block, or guard with 'if GUI_AVAILABLE:' before class definitions.

findings.title: Daemon thread killed immediately on window close
findings.problem: Engine initialization runs in a daemon thread (daemon=True). If the user closes the window during initialization, the thread is killed immediately without cleanup. This could leave the engine in an inconsistent state or leak resources.
findings.fix: Track thread reference and join() on close, or use a shutdown Event for graceful termination. Override destroy() or protocol handler to signal thread shutdown.

findings.title: Conversation history not protected against concurrent access
findings.problem: self.conversation_history is modified in the query callback (potentially running in a thread) while being read elsewhere. If _ask_question() is called while a previous query thread is still appending, data could be corrupted.
findings.fix: Protect conversation_history with threading.Lock, or ensure all modifications go through the message queue.

findings.title: sys._MEIPASS access — correct but LSP-unfriendly pattern
findings.problem: sys._MEIPASS is a PyInstaller-specific attribute. The try/except AttributeError pattern at line 39 is correct, but LSP/static analyzers flag it as 'unknown attribute'. This generates noise in IDE tooling.
findings.fix: Add '# type: ignore[attr-defined]' comment, or use hasattr(sys, '_MEIPASS') pattern which is more explicit.

findings.title: Private API access to CTkScrollableFrame._parent_canvas
findings.problem: self.chat_frame._parent_canvas accesses a private attribute of CTkScrollableFrame. If CustomTkinter changes its internal implementation, this will break silently.
findings.fix: Use public API if available, or document the dependency on CustomTkinter internals.

findings.title: Settings save failure silently printed to stdout
findings.problem: When settings save fails, the error is printed to stdout. On Windows GUI applications, stdout may not be visible to users.
findings.fix: Use messagebox.showwarning() or logger.error() for visible error feedback.

findings.title: SETTINGS_FILE constant defined but never used directly
findings.problem: SETTINGS_FILE = 'app_settings.json' is defined at line 188 but the actual path is computed via _get_settings_path() which uses app_paths module. The constant is misleading.
findings.fix: Either use the constant in _get_settings_path() or remove it.

findings.title: Hardcoded polling interval without named constant
findings.problem: self.after(100, process) uses a hardcoded 100ms polling interval at lines 368, 371. This should be a named constant for clarity.
